text: The surgeon creates a transverse peritoneal incision to enter the preperitoneal space, forming a preperitoneal flap. Dissection proceeds medially towards the pubic bone and Cooper's ligament, preparing the area for mesh placement to reduce hernia recurrence.

The surgeon creates a transverse peritoneal incision and enters the preperitoneal space, forming a preperitoneal flap. Dissection continues medially towards the pubic bone and Cooper's ligament, preparing the area for medial placement of the mesh.
The surgeon focuses on the hernia sac, encountering a significant amount of scar tissue between the cord structures and the hernia sac.

The surgeon uses the enhanced visualization and wristed instrumentation of the robotic platform to perform precise dissection around the hernia sac, carefully protecting the blood vessels to the testicle and the vas deferens.

The surgeon uses the robotic platform to perform a precise dissection around the hernia sac, carefully protecting the blood vessels to the testicle and the vas deferens. The wristed instruments facilitate meticulous dissection, maintaining a bloodless surgical field.
The surgeon performs a precise dissection to separate the hernia sac from the cord structures, including the blood vessels to the testicle, in a patient with a scrotal hernia.

The surgeon carefully dissects the hernia sac away from the vas deferens, using precise techniques to separate these structures.
The surgeon uses robotic instruments to dissect the hernia sac away from the vas deferens, carefully separating the structures to avoid damage.

The surgeon continues the dissection medially to identify the pubic bone and Cooper's ligament, extending the dissection inferiorly, superiorly, and laterally to create an adequate pocket for mesh placement.

The surgeon reduces the preperitoneal fat incarcerated in the hernia defect, then places the mesh into the preperitoneal space. The mesh is laid precisely in the pocket, which is created to match the mesh size, ensuring it covers the hernia defect and prevents movement.

The surgeon secures the mesh to the abdominal wall using bio-glue, followed by closing the peritoneal defect with an absorbable suture. The robotic platform's wristed instrumentation aids in the precise execution of these steps.

The surgeon includes the hernia sac in the repair to reinforce the peritoneum, ensuring additional support to the repaired area.
